blocks.value: It started with a beat-up tape camera and no plan. I filmed everything — the drive, the light, the people who happened to be around. None of it was for anyone. It just felt like the only honest way to hold onto a day.

blocks.caption: The first rig — barely working, always rolling.

blocks.value: Looking back, this is the whole thesis of Kisala Films in one clip: point the camera at your real life and let it become something cinematic later.

blocks.caption: L0ST TAPES — the earliest surviving footage.

blocks.value: You don't need a reason to press record. You need a reason to stop.

blocks.caption: Coast roads after dark — the first real edit.

blocks.value: A trip with no rigid destination turned into the first film I actually cut for other people. Travel and filmmaking stopped being two hobbies and became one practice.

blocks.caption: ANDY Q50 — a build, a driver, and the streets that made it.

blocks.value: Cars and bikes deserved their own recurring format — not a highlight reel, an issue. Each one profiles a machine and the people keeping it alive.
The first commissioned piece. The brief was simple; the standard wasn't. I shot it like short cinema — product, place, and motion, cut to breathe.

blocks.caption: Macro passes — the detail that earns the frame.

blocks.value: It proved a brand film doesn't have to look like an ad. It can look like something you'd actually want to watch.

blocks.title: Field audio — Nairobi mornings
blocks.caption: Audio diary. Player wires in when the file is uploaded.

blocks.value: Some days don't want a camera. They want a microphone and a walk. The life vlogs are the hours between the big shoots — raw, unpolished, honest.

blocks.caption: Cities found, exits missed, no GPS required.

blocks.value: The travel series is the world through a camcorder — graded and cut like film, but shot like a diary. This is the format I'll keep making forever.
Every pin on the globe, every tape in the crate, every entry in this journal — one archive of a life in motion. This is where it all lives now.

blocks.caption: L0ST TAPES — the archive, in motion.
ANDY Q50 — the second tape on the crate.

blocks.value: Go somewhere you can't explain — then come back with the tape.
